entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The security model of Bitcoin, particularly its proof-of-work (PoW) mechanism, plays a crucial role in maintaining the value of the cryptocurrency for all its users. The underlying principle is that the security contributed by PoW benefits everyone who holds Bitcoin, proportionally to their holdings. Consequently, when the generation of Bitcoin blocks is unreliable, it negatively impacts the value of the cryptocurrency across the board.
There is an ongoing debate about the funding of this security. Some argue that charging transaction fees per transaction to fund network security might not be feasible due to fluctuating demand for transactions. If Bitcoin were to rely solely on transaction fees for its security budget, there's a significant risk that insufficient funding could lead to decreased security. This situation could potentially threaten one of the few decentralized methods of storing value, which would be a substantial setback for the digital currency.
For further insights into these issues, more information can be found on Peter Todd’s website at [Peter Todd](https://petertodd.org).
